His overly cheerful comments during the crisis______
his true anxiety about the situation.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Intensified → Made stronger
Revealed → Opposite meaning
Suppressed → Forced down

Q7 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

How may numbers can be made with digits 0, 7, 8 which are greater than ‘0’ and less than a million?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Number of ways to form one digit number = 2
‘0’ cannot be treated as one digit number
Number of ways to form two digit number = 2 × 3 = 6
Number of ways to form three digit number = 2 × 3 × 3 = 18
Number of ways to form four digit number = 2 × 33 = 54
Number of ways to form five digit number = 2 × 34 = 162
Number of ways to foron six digit number = 2 × 35 = 486
Total number of ways = 2 + 6 + 18 + 54 + 162 + 486 = 728

Q9 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Given below are four pairs of words. Identify the one which is different from others.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Murky as well as Dusky – both refer to something which is dark or cheerless – similar in meaning;
Inter means ‘to place’ and exhume means to dig out - opposite in meaning.
Effete means lacking in vigour whereas fructuous means productive or fertile – opposite in meaning.
Benediction is an utterance of good wishes while execration refers to denouncement i.e. both are
opposite in meaning.

If all the above statements are true, which of the following must also be true ?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following alloying elements in steel reduces hardenability and increases ductility?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Silicon and Molybdenum increase the hardenability.
• Niobium, also called columbium, it reduces the hardenability and increases the ductility slightly
which results in a marked increase in impact strength.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is correct regarding Electromagnetic forming?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Electromagnetic forming: This technique is a high energy rate forming (HERF) technique. The
electrical energy first stored in capacitor bank and then discharged through a coil. The coil produces
a magnetic field. The workpiece is in this magnetic field, a current is induced in the job which sets
up its own magnetic field. These magnetic fields repel the workpiece into the die. The workpiece
obviously has to be electrically conductive but need not be magnetic.
